title: PID 1, Signals, Shutdown, Health, Restart, and Resource Constraints · Container Boundary Trace: Docker Graceful Stop Exit
summary: A queue worker ignores termination, accumulates zombies, reports healthy while stalled, and can exhaust host memory. Planned artifact: Add and verify the docker graceful stop exit increment in bounded process lifecycle and health policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Design readiness removal, graceful shutdown, deadlines, forced termination, exit codes, and durable-work recovery.
Trace PID 1 signal handling, child reaping, shell wrappers, init helpers, stop signals, and grace periods.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.

version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment

steps.title: Predict Docker Graceful Stop Exit · Theory Docker Graceful Stop Exit
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory queue worker ignores SIGTERM, leaves child processes, reports healthy while stalled, restarts forever, and consumes 256 MiB. Bound PID 1, health, shutdown, restart, and resources. Competency probe: Design readiness removal, graceful shutdown, deadlines, forced termination, exit codes, and durable-work recovery.

steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory queue worker ignores SIGTERM, leaves child processes, reports healthy while stalled, restarts forever, and consumes 384 MiB. content.text: Design readiness removal, graceful shutdown, deadlines, forced termination, exit codes, and durable-work recovery. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name client, daemon, builder, image, container, host-kernel, storage, network, registry, and supply-chain boundaries; predict state and authority; review the smallest deterministic manifest change; then verify a failure, repair, cleanup, and live-environment transfer limit.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example queue worker ignores SIGTERM, leaves child processes, reports healthy while stalled, restarts forever, and consumes 256 MiB. Bound PID 1, health, shutdown, restart, and resources. Competency probe: Design readiness removal, graceful shutdown, deadlines, forced termination, exit codes, and durable-work recovery. A defensible solution applies Docker Graceful Stop Exit, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to bounded process lifecycle and health policy.

content.code:
# Evidence: docker-worked-docker-process-signals-health-resources-2
  - competency: docker-worked-docker-process-signals-health-resources-2
    artifact: runtime-policy.yaml
    decision: worked docker process signals health resources 2
    scenario-variant: 1
    environment: Docker Desktop VM with synchronized source files
    fault-injection: send termination during an in-flight durable operation
    retained-proof: accessible recovery runbook with rollback rehearsal timing
    case-key: worked2
    change: |
      init: true
      stop_grace_period: 30s
      healthcheck: bounded-readiness-probe
      restart: on-failure:3
      memory: 256m
      pids_limit: 128
    verify: docker inspect training-worker --format json && docker events --since 10m
    failure: Reject an unbounded probe, restart loop as recovery, shell wrapper that swallows signals, or deployment without memory and PID limits.
    repair: Make PID 1 ownership explicit, bound shutdown and health timing, preserve durable work, and test termination plus resource pressure.
content.caption: Runnable Docker artifact and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: A successful docker stop proves the application completed all in-flight work. This fails the Docker Graceful Stop Exit contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.
